seoTitle: How to Write a Book in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to structure a manuscript in Google Docs using heading styles, page breaks, an outline sidebar, and version history for drafts.
intro: Turning scattered notes into a structured, publication-ready manuscript is much easier with the right Google Docs setup. This guide covers configuring page size, using heading styles for chapter navigation, and managing drafts with version history. It's for aspiring authors organizing a book-length project.

steps.heading: Set up your page and typography
steps.body: Go to File then Page Setup to adjust your paper size and margins, then choose clean fonts and spacing for your manuscript.

steps.heading: Apply heading styles to chapters
steps.body: Type your first chapter title, highlight it, and assign it the Heading 1 style so it appears in the navigation outline.

steps.heading: Use the Document Outline sidebar
steps.body: Open the left sidebar Document Outline icon to confirm your chapter links appear and let you jump between sections quickly.

steps.heading: Insert page breaks
steps.body: Place your cursor at the end of a scene, click Insert, hover over Break, and select Page Break so new chapters start on a fresh page.

steps.heading: Add a table of contents and save versions
steps.body: Insert a live Table of Contents in your front matter, then use File, Version History, Name Current Version to freeze safe draft milestones.

faq.question: How do I quickly jump between distant chapters?
faq.answer: Use the Document Outline sidebar, which lists every heading and lets you click to jump straight to that section.

faq.question: Will the table of contents update automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, an inserted Table of Contents updates live as you add or rename headings throughout your manuscript.

faq.question: Can I export my finished manuscript for publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, Google Docs supports exporting to formats like PDF and EPUB for digital publishing platforms.

faq.question: Does Google Docs save my progress automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, changes save automatically to the cloud, and named versions in Version History give you extra milestone checkpoints.
